proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_osimertinib.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.39% on pembrolizumab vs 16.40% off (diff +0.99 percentage points, OR 1.073, chi-square p=3.4e-3). Small but statistically significant overall benefit.
ORR 16.95% on sotorasib vs 16.87% off (diff +0.08 pp, OR 1.006, p=0.83). No overall main effect.
ORR 16.99% on olaparib vs 16.85% off (diff +0.13 pp, OR 1.010, p=0.72). No overall main effect.
ORR 16.55% on osimertinib vs 17.04% off (diff -0.50 pp, OR 0.965, p=0.18). No overall main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_osimertinib on objective_response is larger in patients with egfr_mutation than in those without (positive treatment_osimertinib x egfr_mutation interaction on the log-odds scale).
The benefit of treatment_sotorasib on objective_response is larger in patients with kras_g12c than in those without (positive treatment_sotorasib x kras_g12c interaction on the log-odds scale).

The benefit of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is larger in patients with brca2_mutation than in those without (positive treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction on the log-odds scale).
The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is larger in patients with tmb_high than in those without (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ subgroup: ORR 16.4% on osimertinib vs 16.6% off; EGFR-: 16.6% vs 17.1%. Interaction logOR=+0.024, p=0.76. No predictive effect of EGFR mutation for osimertinib response in this cohort.
KRAS G12C+: ORR 17.5% on sotorasib vs 16.6% off; G12C-: 16.9% vs 16.9%. Interaction logOR=+0.059, p=0.43. No significant predictive effect.
BRCA2+: ORR 14.6% on olaparib vs 16.8% off; BRCA2-: 17.1% vs 16.9%. Interaction logOR=-0.177, p=0.28. Numerically opposite of expected, not significant.
TMB-high: ORR 19.7% on pembro vs 16.7% off; TMB-low: 16.5% vs 16.3%. Interaction logOR=+0.184, p=4.2e-4. TMB-high predicts greater pembro benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with greater treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction).
Patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 derive substantially greater objective_response benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab than patients with pdl1_tps < 0.5.

analyses.result_summary: Continuous interaction: treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps logOR=+0.591, p=1.6e-7. Strong evidence that pembrolizumab benefit increases with PD-L1 TPS.

analyses.code: stratified ORR by pdl1_hi indicator (TPS>=0.5), then logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1>=50%: pembro+ ORR=0.209 vs pembro- ORR=0.156 (diff +5.4 pp, n=10749). PD-L1<50%: pembro+ ORR=0.164 vs pembro- ORR=0.166 (diff -0.2 pp, n=39251). Interaction logOR=+0.376, p=5.0e-11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
stage_iv disease is associated with lower objective_response than non-stage-IV.
has_brain_mets is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
sex_female is associated with higher objective_response than males.

analyses.result_summary: Per-unit ECOG: logOR=-0.375, p=1.7e-93. ECOG 0 ORR ~21.1%, ECOG>0 ~14.6%. Strongest single prognostic factor identified.
Stage IV ORR 15.4% vs non-stage IV 19.6%, logOR=-0.295, p=1.3e-33.
Brain mets ORR 14.4% vs no brain mets 17.8%, logOR=-0.252, p=2.0e-18.
Per percent weight loss: logOR=-0.0385, p=1.8e-32.
Per g/dL albumin: logOR=+0.091, p=1.4e-4.
Per mg/L CRP: logOR=-0.0049, p=6.9e-4.
Female ORR 17.5% vs male 16.4%, logOR=+0.083, p=5.7e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is larger in females than males (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction on log-odds scale).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is larger in squamous than adenocarcinoma histology (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x squamous interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is smaller (or absent) in never smokers compared with ever smokers (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x never_smoker interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with stk11_mutation (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with keap1_mutation (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x keap1_mutation interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR=+0.146, p=2.4e-3. Pembrolizumab benefit is significantly larger in females.
Interaction logOR=+0.030, p=0.58. No significant difference in pembro benefit by histology.
Interaction logOR=-0.072, p=0.29. Not significant; never-smokers do not derive less benefit in this cohort.
Interaction logOR=-0.211, p=1.8e-3. STK11 mutation significantly attenuates pembrolizumab benefit (consistent with known IO resistance signal).
Interaction logOR=-0.058, p=0.36. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with higher ecog_ps (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x ecog_ps interaction on log-odds scale).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with stage_iv disease.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with has_brain_mets.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR=-0.036, p=0.32. ECOG is a strong main-effect prognostic but does not significantly modify pembro benefit.
Interaction logOR=+0.071, p=0.15. Not significant.
Interaction logOR=-0.033, p=0.57. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response (main effect, regardless of treatment).
tmb_high is associated with higher objective_response (main effect, regardless of treatment).
After Bonferroni correction across 23 SNPs, at least one snp_* variant shows a significant association with objective_response (alpha=0.05/23=2.17e-3).

analyses.result_summary: Per-unit (TPS 0->1) logOR=+0.166, p=3.2e-3. Higher PD-L1 expression is associated with higher overall ORR.
TMB-high logOR=+0.127, p=9.8e-7. TMB-high is associated with higher overall ORR.
Smallest p-values: snp_rs7412 p=1.8e-2 (logOR +0.100), snp_rs1050828 p=3.1e-2 (logOR -0.099). No SNP survives Bonferroni alpha 2.17e-3. Conclude no significant SNP main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: objective_response differs by race_ethnicity, with at least one non-white group differing significantly from white.
objective_response differs by insurance_type, with privately insured patients having higher ORR than medicaid (positive private-vs-medicaid contrast).
rural_residence is associated with lower objective_response than non-rural.
Higher education_years is associated with higher objective_response.
treatment_pembrolizumab receipt differs by race_ethnicity (i.e., disparity in IO assignment).

analyses.result_summary: vs reference (asian): black coef +0.002 p=0.97; hispanic -0.040 p=0.49; other +0.055 p=0.56; white -0.083 p=0.10. No race contrast reaches p<0.05.
vs medicaid: medicare +0.052 p=0.16; private +0.077 p=0.040; uninsured +0.063 p=0.35. Privately insured have a small but significant ORR advantage over medicaid.
logOR=-0.021, p=0.49. No significant rural-vs-urban difference.
logOR=-0.003, p=0.40. No significant association.

Pembrolizumab receipt across race groups: asian 50.4%, black 49.0%, hispanic 49.7%, other 50.9%, white 50.2%. Differences are small (range ~2 pp); chi-square not formally significant. No clear allocation disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, age, sex, albumin, weight loss, CRP, race, the treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction remains a positive, significant predictor of objective_response.

In the same multivariable model, treatment_osimertinib x egfr_mutation interaction is NOT a significant positive predictor of objective_response (refuting the conventional matched-targeted-therapy expectation).
In the same multivariable model, treatment_sotorasib x kras_g12c interaction is NOT a significant positive predictor of objective_response.
In the same multivariable model, treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction is NOT a significant positive predictor of objective_response.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + age_years + sex_female + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + crp_mg_l + treatment_pembrolizumab*pdl1_tps + treatment_pembrolizumab*tmb_high + treatment_pembrolizumab*stk11_mutation + treatment_osimertinib*egfr_mutation + treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + C(race_ethnicity)')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps logOR=+0.595, p=1.7e-7. Hypothesis supported. Adjusted main pembro effect logOR=-0.131 (p=5e-3) reflecting that without TPS+ amplification, pembro provides no benefit.

analyses.code: same multivariable model -- coefficient on treatment_osimertinib:egfr_mutation
analyses.result_summary: treatment_osimertinib:egfr_mutation logOR=+0.025, p=0.75. Refutes the standard matched-targeted-therapy expectation in this dataset.

analyses.code: same multivariable model -- coefficient on treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c
analyses.result_summary: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c logOR=+0.064, p=0.39. Refutes the matched-targeted-therapy expectation.

analyses.code: same multivariable model -- coefficient on treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation logOR=-0.178, p=0.28 (numerically opposite direction). Refutes the matched-targeted-therapy expectation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, heart failure, CAD, AF, VTE, autoimmune, hepatitis B/C, HIV, prior_malignancy, depression/anxiety, ILD), at least one is significantly associated (p<0.05) with objective_response.
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response (a patient-reported symptom prognostic).
There is a 3-way interaction such that pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in pdl1_hi patients without stk11_mutation (negative treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_hi:stk11_mutation term).

analyses.result_summary: Only coronary_artery_disease reaches p<0.05 (logOR=-0.062, p=0.049). All other comorbidities NS. Conclusion: comorbidities are largely uninformative for ORR in this cohort.
logOR=-0.022, p=0.040. Higher fatigue grade modestly predicts lower ORR. Other PRO grades (pain, dyspnea, cough, appetite_loss) NS.

Stratified ORR (pembro+ vs pembro-): pdl1_hi=0,stk11=0: 16.6 vs 16.5 (+0.1 pp); pdl1_hi=0,stk11=1: 15.6 vs 17.4 (-1.8 pp); pdl1_hi=1,stk11=0: 21.7 vs 15.4 (+6.4 pp); pdl1_hi=1,stk11=1: 16.3 vs 16.8 (-0.5 pp). 3-way interaction logOR=-0.322, p=0.049. Pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in PD-L1-high / STK11-WT; STK11 mutation eliminates the PD-L1-high advantage.
